claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex. He's 8 years old and lives with me here in the UK. I'd like to know if I'm eligible for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know that you have an 8-year-old son named Alex who lives with you in the UK.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask a few more questions:

**Are you responsible for Alex's care? By this I mean, do you provide for his day-to-day needs such as food, clothes, and other essentials?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm fully responsible for Alex. I take care of all his day-to-day needs - his food, clothes, school things, everything he needs really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for confirming that. 

Now, **can you confirm that you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I can confirm that. I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for that information.

One final question: **Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or any residential accommodation, or does he live with you full-time at home?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He lives with me at home, though I should mention he was in care for a short period recently - just a couple of months - but he's back home with me now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that information. That's helpful to know.

To clarify: **Is Alex currently still in care, or is he now back home with you permanently? And approximately how long ago did he return home?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's back home with me now. He returned home recently - it was about 7 weeks total that he was in care, and he's been back with me since then.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. So Alex was in care for 7 weeks total and is now back home with you.

One last question to complete the assessment: **While Alex was in care during those 7 weeks, was the local council paying towards his accommodation or maintenance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council wasn't paying towards his accommodation or maintenance during that time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good News - You Are Eligible!

**You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

✓ **Alex meets the age requirement** - He's 8 years old, which is under the age limit of 16 (or 20 if in approved education or training)

✓ **You are responsible for Alex** - You provide for all his day-to-day needs including food, clothes, and essentials

✓ **You both live in the UK** - Both you and Alex reside in the UK

✓ **No one else is claiming** - Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex

✓ **The care period is within limits** - Although Alex was in care for 7 weeks, this is under the 8-week threshold that would affect your Child Benefit payments. Since the local council wasn't paying towards his accommodation or maintenance, and he's now back home with you, this doesn't impact your eligibility.

You can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Alex. If you haven't already claimed, you should do so as soon as possible. If you had been receiving Child Benefit before Alex went into care, your payments should continue unaffected since he was away for less than 8 weeks.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which means he is under 16, meeting the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant lives in the UK and is fully responsible for Alex's day-to-day care, including providing food, clothes, and other essentials. Alex lives with the claimant at home in the UK. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. While Alex was in care for 7 weeks recently, this is under the 8-week threshold mentioned in the rules, and the local council was not paying towards his accommodation or maintenance during that time. He is now back home living with the claimant. Based on these circumstances, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for Alex as they are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them in the UK.

Alex is now back home. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
